{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Administrative and Finance Manager",
  "description": "Catering to Africans In Need (CAIN) is a Non-governmental organization focused on providing improved education and healthcare to the less privileged in our society.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Administrative and Finance Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time&nbsp;

Key Responsibilities
Administrative Responsibilities:


	Office management&nbsp;&ndash; Ensure the smooth day-to-day running of the office, including supplies, utilities, and facilities.
	Staff support and HR&nbsp;&ndash; Maintain personnel records, support onboarding/offboarding, track leave, and ensure policy compliance.
	Vendor and contract management&nbsp;&ndash; Oversee procurement, manage service contracts, and ensure timely payments.
	Documentation and compliance&nbsp;&ndash; Maintain up-to-date organizational documents, licenses, and policies.
	Support leadership&nbsp;&ndash; Assist senior management with research, fundraising, strategic planning, execution, reporting, and evaluation of programs.
	Coordinate logistics&nbsp;&ndash; Oversee travel, meetings, trainings, and other internal events.
	IT and asset management&nbsp;&ndash; Track office assets and support basic tech or liaise with vendors for support.
	Ensure timely and effective communication across teams&nbsp;&ndash; Facilitate smooth internal coordination to drive efficient project and program delivery.


Finance Responsibilities:


	Prepare and manage budgets&nbsp;&ndash; Develop annual budgets, monitor expenditures, and ensure alignment with organizational goals.
	Maintain accurate financial records&nbsp;&ndash; Record transactions, manage ledgers, and oversee financial reporting.
	Generate financial reports&nbsp;&ndash; Prepare monthly, quarterly, and annual reports for management, donors, and regulatory bodies.
	Ensure regulatory compliance&nbsp;&ndash; File tax returns, process payroll, and ensure compliance with statutory requirements (e.g., SCUML, FIRS, LIRS, pension, etc.).
	Manage audits&nbsp;&ndash; Liaise with external auditors, prepare audit documents, and implement recommendations.
	Oversee cash flow and bank reconciliations&nbsp;&ndash; Monitor bank accounts, manage petty cash, and ensure healthy liquidity.
	Support grant reporting&nbsp;&ndash; Track donor funds, prepare financial reports for grants, and ensure proper fund allocation.


Requirements


	A Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in&nbsp;Accounting,&nbsp;Finance, or a related field (minimum 2:1).
	Must have&nbsp;completed NYSC.
	1&ndash;2 years of experience&nbsp;working in an&nbsp;Accounting or Audit firm.
	Strong understanding of accounting systems, budgeting, and compliance.
	Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to effectively engage a wide range of stakeholders, including partners, donors, staff, students, and volunteers.
	Patience and the ability to build positive and supportive relationships with teenagers.
	Excellent organizational, multitasking, and problem-solving abilities.
	High proficiency in Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Slides, Drive) and other administrative software and tools.
	Strong written and verbal communication skills.
	Mandatory availability to work every Saturday&nbsp;to support program activities.
	A genuine passion for community impact and a strong commitment to empowering underserved children and youth through education.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and a compelling cover letter detailing their experience and motivation for joining CAIN Educational Foundation to:&nbsp;info@cainafrica.org&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Administrative and Finance Manager Job at Catering to Africans In Need (CAIN)




        
    

        
        Catering to Africans In Need (CAIN) is a Non-governmental organization focused on providing improved education and healthcare to the less privileged in our society.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Administrative and Finance Manager

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time 

Key Responsibilities
Administrative Responsibilities:
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Office management – Ensure the smooth day-to-day running of the office, including supplies, utilities, and facilities.
	Staff support and HR – Maintain personnel records, support onboarding/offboarding, track leave, and ensure policy compliance.
	Vendor and contract management – Oversee procurement, manage service contracts, and ensure timely payments.
	Documentation and compliance – Maintain up-to-date organizational documents, licenses, and policies.
	Support leadership – Assist senior management with research, fundraising, strategic planning, execution, reporting, and evaluation of programs.
	Coordinate logistics – Oversee travel, meetings, trainings, and other internal events.
	IT and asset management – Track office assets and support basic tech or liaise with vendors for support.
	Ensure timely and effective communication across teams – Facilitate smooth internal coordination to drive efficient project and program delivery.


Finance Responsibilities:


	Prepare and manage budgets – Develop annual budgets, monitor expenditures, and ensure alignment with organizational goals.
	Maintain accurate financial records – Record transactions, manage ledgers, and oversee financial reporting.
	Generate financial reports – Prepare monthly, quarterly, and annual reports for management, donors, and regulatory bodies.
	Ensure regulatory compliance – File tax returns, process payroll, and ensure compliance with statutory requirements (e.g., SCUML, FIRS, LIRS, pension, etc.).
	Manage audits – Liaise with external auditors, prepare audit documents, and implement recommendations.
	Oversee cash flow and bank reconciliations – Monitor bank accounts, manage petty cash, and ensure healthy liquidity.
	Support grant reporting – Track donor funds, prepare financial reports for grants, and ensure proper fund allocation.


Requirements


	A Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field (minimum 2:1).
	Must have completed NYSC.
	1–2 years of experience working in an Accounting or Audit firm.
	Strong understanding of accounting systems, budgeting, and compliance.
	Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to effectively engage a wide range of stakeholders, including partners, donors, staff, students, and volunteers.
	Patience and the ability to build positive and supportive relationships with teenagers.
	Excellent organizational, multitasking, and problem-solving abilities.
	High proficiency in Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Slides, Drive) and other administrative software and tools.
	Strong written and verbal communication skills.
	Mandatory availability to work every Saturday to support program activities.
	A genuine passion for community impact and a strong commitment to empowering underserved children and youth through education.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and a compelling cover letter detailing their experience and motivation for joining CAIN Educational Foundation to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "IT Consultant",
  "description": "ARO (pronounced A-R-O) is a dynamic and versatile company offering exceptional services in both the property and technology sectors. Through our specialised divisions&mdash;ARO Management, ARO Properties, and ARO Technologies&mdash;we&#39;re dedicated to blending innovation and expertise to meet our clients&#39; diverse needs seamlessly. Founded in 2015. With a focus on providing exceptional stays, we soon realized our talent for hospitality and expanded into property management services. By 2018, we launched ARO Management Nigeria Ltd, tackling the challenges of property management in a new environment.?

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: IT Consultant

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type:&nbsp;Contract (3 months, with possible extension)

Role Summary


	We are looking for a proactive and solution-driven IT Consultant to join our team.
	This role is ideal for someone who enjoys working with clients, solving real business problems, and building practical tech solutions.
	You should be confident, curious, and able to show real projects you have worked on.


Key Responsibilities


	Work closely with internal teams and clients to understand needs and deliver IT solutions
	Build and manage websites using WIX Studio, WordPress, and Lovable
	Use AI tools such as ChatGPT, Gemini, and Replit to improve workflows
	Create automation processes using Make.com, Zapier, and similar platforms
	Test, improve, and implement systems that increase efficiency
	Support small projects from start to finish
	Research and suggest better tools and ways of working.


Requirements


	Minimum of a 2:1 degree (Computer Science preferred but not compulsory)
	Must have a working laptop
	Must be able to show a portfolio of projects/products built
	Experience working on real-life projects or with clients
	Good knowledge of WIX Studio and WordPress
	Experience or familiarity with Lovable or similar tools
	Familiar with AI tools (ChatGPT, Gemini, etc.)
	Basic understanding of automation tools (Make.com, Zapier)
	Strong problem-solving skills
	Able to work independently and take initiative
	Must provide references.


Ideal Candidate:


	Confident and comfortable working with people
	Good communication skills
	Curious and eager to learn
	Able to think independently and solve problems.
	Organized and reliable.


Salary
N120,000 -&nbsp;N130,000 monthly (based on experience).

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;team@aromgmt.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

IT Consultant Job at ARO Management Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        ARO (pronounced A-R-O) is a dynamic and versatile company offering exceptional services in both the property and technology sectors. Through our specialised divisions—ARO Management, ARO Properties, and ARO Technologies—we're dedicated to blending innovation and expertise to meet our clients' diverse needs seamlessly. Founded in 2015. With a focus on providing exceptional stays, we soon realized our talent for hospitality and expanded into property management services. By 2018, we launched ARO Management Nigeria Ltd, tackling the challenges of property management in a new environment.?

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: IT Consultant
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Contract (3 months, with possible extension)

Role Summary


	We are looking for a proactive and solution-driven IT Consultant to join our team.
	This role is ideal for someone who enjoys working with clients, solving real business problems, and building practical tech solutions.
	You should be confident, curious, and able to show real projects you have worked on.


Key Responsibilities


	Work closely with internal teams and clients to understand needs and deliver IT solutions
	Build and manage websites using WIX Studio, WordPress, and Lovable
	Use AI tools such as ChatGPT, Gemini, and Replit to improve workflows
	Create automation processes using Make.com, Zapier, and similar platforms
	Test, improve, and implement systems that increase efficiency
	Support small projects from start to finish
	Research and suggest better tools and ways of working.


Requirements


	Minimum of a 2:1 degree (Computer Science preferred but not compulsory)
	Must have a working laptop
	Must be able to show a portfolio of projects/products built
	Experience working on real-life projects or with clients
	Good knowledge of WIX Studio and WordPress
	Experience or familiarity with Lovable or similar tools
	Familiar with AI tools (ChatGPT, Gemini, etc.)
	Basic understanding of automation tools (Make.com, Zapier)
	Strong problem-solving skills
	Able to work independently and take initiative
	Must provide references.


Ideal Candidate:


	Confident and comfortable working with people
	Good communication skills
	Curious and eager to learn
	Able to think independently and solve problems.
	Organized and reliable.


Salary
N120,000 - N130,000 monthly (based on experience).

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Soft Services Manager",
  "description": "CBRE is a global leader in real estate services and investment. With a presence in over 100 countries, we provide comprehensive solutions to real estate owners, investors, and occupiers. Our mission is to enable our clients to maximize the value of their real estate assets through innovative strategies and top-notch services.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Soft Services Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	The Soft Services Manager, based in Lagos Nigeria is responsible for overseeing the team that delivers all aspects of cleaning, grounds maintenance, vending services, catering and client liaison within the data centre.
	As well as planning hand service PPM visits, ensuring compliance with the Master Service Agreement (MSA).
	The role requires a hands-on approach to managing service providers, maintaining high standards, and ensuring a seamless experience for clients and stakeholders.


Key Responsibilities
Soft Services Management:


	Oversee cleaning, landscaping, waste management, and vending services to maintain a high standard of hygiene and operational efficiency.
	Conduct regular inspections to ensure compliance with MSA and service level agreements (SLAs).
	Ensure proper waste disposal and recycling procedures are followed according to environmental standards


Vendor &amp; Contractor Management:


	Manage third-party service providers, ensuring contractual obligations and performance standards are met.
	Conduct regular audits and monitor KPIs for all soft service contracts.
	Handle escalations and resolve service-related issues promptly.


Client Liaison &amp; Stakeholder Engagement:


	Act as the primary point of contact for clients regarding soft service-related queries, feedback, and concerns.
	Ensure client expectations are met and exceeded by maintaining proactive communication.
	Support client site visits and audits to ensure satisfaction and compliance.


Health, Safety &amp; Compliance:


	Ensure all soft service activities comply with health and safety regulations and data centre security protocols.
	Conduct risk assessments for cleaning chemicals, equipment, and vendor operations.
	Maintain incident and compliance reports, addressing any issues promptly


Continuous Improvement &amp; Reporting:


	Identify opportunities for process improvement and cost optimization in soft services.
	Maintain accurate records of service activities, contractor performance, and compliance audits.
	Provide monthly reports to management on soft service operations, performance, and improvements.


Required Skills &amp; Qualifications


	Planning of Hard service PPM and Reactive works.
	Ensure all planned work according to client schedule is submitted in time.
	Ensure RAMS are reviewed ad correct before submission to ensure we meet the client deadlines.


Essential:


	Proven track record of managing soft services (cleaning, grounds maintenance, vending) within a data centre, corporate, or critical environment.
	Minimum of 5 years of experience in a similar role within facility management, property services, or data centre operations.
	Strong understanding of MSA, SLAs, and KPI management for service contracts.
	Experience in client engagement and stakeholder management.
	Knowledge of health, safety, and environmental compliance within a high-security environment.


Preferred:


	Qualifications in Facilities Management, Soft Services, or related fields.
	Experience working within a mission-critical environment.
	Cleanng, and chemical and machine handling certifications
	Soft service platform experience


Key Competencies:


	Strong leadership and vendor management skills
	Excellent problem-solving and decision-making abilities
	Ability to manage multiple service providers and contracts
	Strong communication and client relationship skills
	Proactive, detail-oriented, and results-driven
",
  "datePosted": "2026-03-26",
  "validThrough": "2026-04-27",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "CBRE"
    
  },

Soft Services Manager Job at CBRE




        
    

        
        CBRE is a global leader in real estate services and investment. With a presence in over 100 countries, we provide comprehensive solutions to real estate owners, investors, and occupiers. Our mission is to enable our clients to maximize the value of their real estate assets through innovative strategies and top-notch services.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Soft Services Manager

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Soft Services Manager, based in Lagos Nigeria is responsible for overseeing the team that delivers all aspects of cleaning, grounds maintenance, vending services, catering and client liaison within the data centre.
	As well as planning hand service PPM visits, ensuring compliance with the Master Service Agreement (MSA).
	The role requires a hands-on approach to managing service providers, maintaining high standards, and ensuring a seamless experience for clients and stakeholders.


Key Responsibilities
Soft Services Management:


	Oversee cleaning, landscaping, waste management, and vending services to maintain a high standard of hygiene and operational efficiency.
	Conduct regular inspections to ensure compliance with MSA and service level agreements (SLAs).
	Ensure proper waste disposal and recycling procedures are followed according to environmental standards


Vendor & Contractor Management:


	Manage third-party service providers, ensuring contractual obligations and performance standards are met.
	Conduct regular audits and monitor KPIs for all soft service contracts.
	Handle escalations and resolve service-related issues promptly.


Client Liaison & Stakeholder Engagement:


	Act as the primary point of contact for clients regarding soft service-related queries, feedback, and concerns.
	Ensure client expectations are met and exceeded by maintaining proactive communication.
	Support client site visits and audits to ensure satisfaction and compliance.


Health, Safety & Compliance:


	Ensure all soft service activities comply with health and safety regulations and data centre security protocols.
	Conduct risk assessments for cleaning chemicals, equipment, and vendor operations.
	Maintain incident and compliance reports, addressing any issues promptly


Continuous Improvement & Reporting:


	Identify opportunities for process improvement and cost optimization in soft services.
	Maintain accurate records of service activities, contractor performance, and compliance audits.
	Provide monthly reports to management on soft service operations, performance, and improvements.


Required Skills & Qualifications


	Planning of Hard service PPM and Reactive works.
	Ensure all planned work according to client schedule is submitted in time.
	Ensure RAMS are reviewed ad correct before submission to ensure we meet the client deadlines.


Essential:


	Proven track record of managing soft services (cleaning, grounds maintenance, vending) within a data centre, corporate, or critical environment.
	Minimum of 5 years of experience in a similar role within facility management, property services, or data centre operations.
	Strong understanding of MSA, SLAs, and KPI management for service contracts.
	Experience in client engagement and stakeholder management.
	Knowledge of health, safety, and environmental compliance within a high-security environment.


Preferred:


	Qualifications in Facilities Management, Soft Services, or related fields.
	Experience working within a mission-critical environment.
	Cleanng, and chemical and machine handling certifications
	Soft service platform experience


Key Competencies:


	Strong leadership and vendor management skills
	Excellent problem-solving and decision-making abilities
	Ability to manage multiple service providers and contracts
	Strong communication and client relationship skills
	Proactive, detail-oriented, and results-driven

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Administrative Assistant",
  "description": "Tribest is a corporate support solutions provider with a comprehensive range of people outsourcing services. We have a diversified team of management, strategy, banking and capacity development experts working together to provide organizations with the best human resource management solutions. With a team that diverse, we are setting new standards of performance and raising the bar in excellent service delivery.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Administrative Assistant

Job Location:&nbsp;Victoria Island, Lekki and Obalende - Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	An Administrative Assistant job description involves providing essential clerical and organizational support, handling tasks like managing schedules, answering phones, drafting correspondence, organizing files, coordinating meetings, and managing office supplies to ensure smooth daily operations for staff and executives, requiring strong communication, multitasking, and computer skills.
	Administrative Assistants are the central hub for office communication, keeping teams productive and organized through a variety of clerical and organizational tasks managing complex schedules, handling sensitive information with discretion, and ensuring all administrative functions run efficiently.


Key Responsibilities


	Scheduling &amp; Coordination:&nbsp;Manage calendars, book appointments, arrange meetings, and coordinate travel.
	Communication:&nbsp;Answer phones, respond to emails, greet visitors, and route messages.
	Document Management:&nbsp;Prepare, edit, and proofread reports, memos and other documents;maintain filing systems.
	Office Operations:&nbsp;Order supplies, manage inventory, handle incoming/outgoing mail, and maintain office equipment.
	Support:&nbsp;Assist with data entry, basic bookkeeping, and provide general support to managers and staff.


Essential Skills &amp; Qualifications


	HND or BSc required.
	Conversant with Microsoft Office
	Excellent verbal and written communication.
	Strong organizational and time-management abilities.
	Proficiency with computers (spreadsheets, presentations, databases).
	Problem-solving and decision-making.
	Positive attitude and ability to work with others.
",

Administrative Assistant Job at Tribest Corporate Support Limited




        
    

        
        Tribest is a corporate support solutions provider with a comprehensive range of people outsourcing services. We have a diversified team of management, strategy, banking and capacity development experts working together to provide organizations with the best human resource management solutions. With a team that diverse, we are setting new standards of performance and raising the bar in excellent service delivery.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Administrative Assistant

Job Location: Victoria Island, Lekki and Obalende - Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	An Administrative Assistant job description involves providing essential clerical and organizational support, handling tasks like managing schedules, answering phones, drafting correspondence, organizing files, coordinating meetings, and managing office supplies to ensure smooth daily operations for staff and executives, requiring strong communication, multitasking, and computer skills.
	Administrative Assistants are the central hub for office communication, keeping teams productive and organized through a variety of clerical and organizational tasks managing complex schedules, handling sensitive information with discretion, and ensuring all administrative functions run efficiently.


Key Responsibilities


	Scheduling & Coordination: Manage calendars, book appointments, arrange meetings, and coordinate travel.
	Communication: Answer phones, respond to emails, greet visitors, and route messages.
	Document Management: Prepare, edit, and proofread reports, memos and other documents;maintain filing systems.
	Office Operations: Order supplies, manage inventory, handle incoming/outgoing mail, and maintain office equipment.
	Support: Assist with data entry, basic bookkeeping, and provide general support to managers and staff.


Essential Skills & Qualifications


	HND or BSc required.
	Conversant with Microsoft Office
	Excellent verbal and written communication.
	Strong organizational and time-management abilities.
	Proficiency with computers (spreadsheets, presentations, databases).
	Problem-solving and decision-making.
	Positive attitude and ability to work with others.

  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Business Manager",
  "description": "Work Dey HR Services - Our client, a fast-growing construction company, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Apo, Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	Our client is seeking a dynamic and results-driven Business Manager to oversee operations, drive growth, and ensure the smooth running of our business activities.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and implement business strategies to achieve company goals.
	Oversee daily operations, ensuring efficiency and compliance with industry standards.
	Manage budgets, financial planning, and reporting.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with clients, partners, and stakeholders.
	Lead and motivate teams to deliver high-quality projects on time and within budget.
	Identify new business opportunities and drive expansion initiatives.


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Business Administration, Management, or related field (MBA is an added advantage).
	Minimum of 5 years of experience in contract procurement, project management in similar industry, Excellent communication and presentation is highly required.
	Strong leadership, organizational, and communication skills.
	Proven track record in financial management and business development.
	Ability to work under pressure and deliver results in a fast-paced environment.


Skills:


	Must have excellent IT skills in Microsoft word,excel, PowerPoint and at least 2 google workspace.


Salary
N150,000 - N250,000 / month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;career.wsl@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Business Manager Job at Work Dey HR Services




        
    

        
        Work Dey HR Services - Our client, a fast-growing construction company, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Manager

Job Location: Apo, Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	Our client is seeking a dynamic and results-driven Business Manager to oversee operations, drive growth, and ensure the smooth running of our business activities.


Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Develop and implement business strategies to achieve company goals.
	Oversee daily operations, ensuring efficiency and compliance with industry standards.
	Manage budgets, financial planning, and reporting.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with clients, partners, and stakeholders.
	Lead and motivate teams to deliver high-quality projects on time and within budget.
	Identify new business opportunities and drive expansion initiatives.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Management, or related field (MBA is an added advantage).
	Minimum of 5 years of experience in contract procurement, project management in similar industry, Excellent communication and presentation is highly required.
	Strong leadership, organizational, and communication skills.
	Proven track record in financial management and business development.
	Ability to work under pressure and deliver results in a fast-paced environment.


Skills:


	Must have excellent IT skills in Microsoft word,excel, PowerPoint and at least 2 google workspace.


Salary
N150,000 - N250,000 / month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply
